description: This endpoint provides comprehensive information about all the pools on the selected chain, including details about its assets, the contract address of its liquidity pool, and other relevant data. This information is crucial for making off-chain calculations and predicting quotes.

parameters.name: chainId
parameters.description: ID of the blockchain network

parameters.name: pool_address
parameters.description: The address of the pool. If not provided, all pools on the selected chain will be returned.

parameters.name: time_in_seconds
parameters.description: The time duration for which a quote will be available. This value is used to calculate the fees; as you decrease the time, you will see lower fees. By default, the system sets a predefined value. Note: For on-chain data, this field is not considered.

parameters.name: fieldset
parameters.description: Determines whether the API should make calls to the blockchain to return on-chain data. If the parameter is not provided, it will default to handling on-chain data.

description: This endpoint facilitates the creation of a potential quote for executing a swap between two assets. It allows users to obtain pricing information and other essential details necessary to initiate asset swaps within the system. Use this endpoint to explore the potential cost and feasibility of asset exchanges before finalizing transactions.

parameters.name: chainId
parameters.description: ID of the blockchain network

parameters.name: pool_address
parameters.description: Address of the pool. If not present, the best pool will be calculated

parameters.name: time_in_seconds
parameters.description: Amount of time between accepting a quote and receiving the quote on the blockchain. As this value increases, the quote will get worse for the user.

parameters.name: output_amount
parameters.description: Desired quantity of assets to receive as part of the swap. This field is mutually exclusive with `input_amount`

parameters.name: input_amount
parameters.description: Desired quantity of assets you are willing to exchange. This field is mutually exclusive with `output_amount`

parameters.name: input_asset_symbol
parameters.description: Identifier of the asset you want to provide for the swap. Should correspond to the asset name returned from the pool call

parameters.name: output_asset_symbol
parameters.description: Identifier of the asset you want to receive in the swap. Should correspond to the asset name returned from the pool call

summary: Sign a quote for swap execution
description: This endpoint plays a pivotal role in the preparation and execution of secure asset swaps on the blockchain. By providing a Quote ID and a destination address, users can initiate the signing process, resulting in the generation of the essential signature and transaction data. This signature and data package, in turn, is integral for ensuring the validity and security of asset transfers within the blockchain network.

summary: Quote and sign in one request
description: This endpoint streamlines the swap process by combining the quote generation and signing steps into a single request. Users can obtain real-time pricing information for an asset swap and simultaneously receive the required signature and transaction data for execution on the blockchain. This unified flow reduces latency, simplifies integration, and ensures secure, ready-to-broadcast transactions without the need for multiple API calls.

parameters.name: chainId
parameters.description: ID of the blockchain network

parameters.name: pool_address
parameters.description: Address of the pool. If not present, the best pool will be calculated

parameters.name: time_in_seconds
parameters.description: Amount of time between accepting a quote and receiving the quote on the blockchain. As this value increases, the quote will get worse for the user.

parameters.name: output_amount
parameters.description: Desired quantity of assets to receive as part of the swap. This field is mutually exclusive with `input_amount`

parameters.name: input_amount
parameters.description: Desired quantity of assets you are willing to exchange. This field is mutually exclusive with `output_amount`

parameters.name: input_asset_symbol
parameters.description: Identifier of the asset you want to provide for the swap. Should correspond to the asset name returned from the pool call

parameters.name: output_asset_symbol
parameters.description: Identifier of the asset you want to receive in the swap. Should correspond to the asset name returned from the pool call

parameters.name: destination_address
parameters.description: Address that will receive the output token

parameters.name: sender_address
parameters.description: For DEX aggregator partners that are using their own smart contract to mediate the interaction between users and Blade. Aggregators should use the address of the account that they will pull tokens from (i.e., the EOA user address) as opposed to the address of their deployed contract. By default, `destination_address` will be used.

parameters.name: aux_data
parameters.description: For use with the calldata swap feature, it can be set to any string and is used for identification purposes in the event logs. By default is `0x436c697070657200000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000` that is the representation of `Clipper`

parameters.name: calldata
parameters.description: Send this as true if we want to get the bytes representation of the swap in the response. Client can use this to be sent directly to the pool contract for execution.

description: This endpoint allows users to deposit assets into a liquidity pool by submitting the required details, such as the pool contract address and the amount of assets to be deposited. This process is crucial for users who want to increase the liquidity of the pool and potentially earn rewards or fees. Use this endpoint to securely and efficiently add assets to the pool, contributing to the stability and performance of the ecosystem.
By passing in the appropriate options, you can generate a swap quote
By passing in the appropriate options, you can generate a swap transaction
By passing in a chainId, you can get dollar prices for tokens addresses

summary: returns price for specified chainId and token
description: By passing in a chainId and address, you can get a dollar price for specified token address

summary: returns token data for specified chainId and token
description: By passing in a chainId and address, you can get token data for specified token address

summary: Returns all available liquidity providers for the specified chainId
description: By passing in a chainId you can get the enabled liquidity providers.
